text: Object detection in remote sensing imagery is still a difficult task, owing to large amounts of background noise and limited pixel information in the images, which severely limits the performance of detectors. To improve the performance of object detection in remote sensing images, we propose a novel detection network called LAR-YOLOv8 based on YOLOv8. To this end, the improvements made to the network include the following three points: 1) In the feature network part, the enhanced network is obtained by combining the dual-branch attention mechanism to enhance the local features, the vision transformer block (ViTB16) to strengthen the global features, so as to effectively learn and obtain more rich and strong features. 2) In the structure part, an attention-guided bidirectional feature pyramid network (ABFPN) is proposed by utilizing the dynamic sparse attention mechanism in shallow layers and adding more top-down paths to guide the feature fusion. 3) In the loss part, a novel loss function named RIOU loss is proposed to prevent gradient loss and strengthen the model's shape awareness for the predictive bounding box and the ground-truth bounding box. Experimental results conducted on NWPU VHR-10, RSOD, and CARPK datasets demonstrate that LAR-YOLOv8 achieves remarkable improvement in terms of mAP and small object detection performance while maintaining reasonable parameters and speed.
explanation: Highly coherent and technically plausible with specific dataset references. AI generation is plausible but not certain, and there are no obvious AI-style artifacts.
